target.name: Sofar Ocean
target.founder: Tim Janssen, Brad Lassey, Alex Wight

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $75M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Sofar Ocean is a Series B ocean and maritime technology company that has raised $75M+ to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of public comparables and identified private peers in the ocean tech sector, limiting triangulation to a single methodology.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $75M total capital raised as a valuation proxy, yielding a midpoint valuation of $142M. This method reflects market-observed step-ups between funding rounds but relies on the assumption that total raise correlates meaningfully to enterprise value at this stage. The resulting range spans $121M (low) to $164M (high), with $142M as the central estimate.

The narrow methodological foundation represents a material limitation. Without comparable public or private transaction data in ocean technology, the analysis cannot validate whether the 1.9x multiplier appropriately reflects Sofar Ocean's specific risk, growth trajectory, or market conditions. Sensitivity to the multiplier assumption is high—a 1.5x step-up would imply $112.5M; a 2.3x step-up would suggest $172.5M. Further valuation confidence would require either identification of relevant sector peers or additional methodologies anchored to revenue, customer concentration, or technology-specific metrics not present in the available data.
